question: How can we validate synthetic lethal gene pairs from Feng et al. (2022) and identify druggable opportunities for TP53-mutant cancers?
revision: v2 — gap-fill run addressing 5 quality review findings

nodes.label: TOP Trial - Osimertinib +/- Carboplatin/Pemetrexed for EGFR+TP53 NSCLC
TRUMP Study - NGS-directed therapy for advanced NSCLC

properties.note: Top TP53-associated disease in Open Targets
Most relevant cancer type for NCT04695925 (NSCLC with TP53 mutations)

edges.evidence: BioGRID genetic interaction confirmed (TP53 has 388 genetic interactions, TYMS confirmed as partner)
ChEMBL /mechanism endpoint: 'Thymidylate synthase inhibitor' (mec_id=9637, target_chembl=CHEMBL1952, direct_interaction=1, disease_efficacy=1, max_phase=4, FDA ref: label/2004/021677lbl.pdf)
ChEMBL CHEMBL:185 (5-fluorouracil), approved TYMS inhibitor, max_phase=4
ClinicalTrials.gov NCT04695925: Phase III RCT of Osimertinib + Carboplatin/Pemetrexed for EGFR+TP53 NSCLC (verified via clinicaltrials_get_trial)
ClinicalTrials.gov NCT03574402: Phase II umbrella study of NGS-directed therapy for advanced NSCLC (includes Pemetrexed arm, verified via clinicaltrials_get_trial)

gap_fill_summary.gap1_orcs: SKIPPED — BioGRID ORCS API returned empty responses (no API key available or endpoint unreachable)
gap_fill_summary.gap2_5fu_trials: COMPLETED — 12 trials found for 'TP53 fluorouracil'; none directly test TP53-TYMS synthetic lethality with 5-FU
gap_fill_summary.gap3_mechanism: COMPLETED — ChEMBL /mechanism confirms Pemetrexed as Thymidylate synthase inhibitor (CHEMBL1952) + DHFR inhibitor (CHEMBL202) + GAR transformylase inhibitor (CHEMBL3972)
gap_fill_summary.gap4_nct03574402: COMPLETED — Verified: Phase II umbrella study, NGS-directed therapy, includes Pemetrexed arm, status UNKNOWN, completed 2022
gap_fill_summary.gap5_open_targets: COMPLETED — TP53 (ENSG00000141510) has 3,277 disease associations; top: Li-Fraumeni syndrome (0.876), hepatocellular carcinoma (0.796), lung adenocarcinoma (0.729)
